div.p: and it will exalt him in glory.1 He will then take a higher exaltation, and I will take his place, and thereby become exalted myself.2 So that Jesus treads in the tracks of his Father, and inherits what God did before;3 and God is thus glorified4 and exalted in the salvation and exaltation of all his children.5 It is plain beyond disputation, and you thus learn some of the first principles of the Gospel,6 about which so much hath been said.

When you climb up a ladder, you must begin at the bottom, and ascend step by step, until you arrive at the top; and so it is with the principles of the Gospel—you must begin with the first, and go on until you learn all the principles of exaltation. But it will be a great while after you have passed through the veil7 before you will have learned them. It is not all to be comprehended in this world; it will be a great work to learn our salvation8 and exaltation even beyond the grave. I suppose I am not allowed to go into an investigation of anything that is not contained in the Bible. If I do, I think there are so many over-wise9 men here, that they would cry “treason”10 and put me to death.11 So I will go to the old Bible and turn commentator today.

I shall comment on the very first Hebrew word in the Bible;12 I will make a comment on the very first sentence of the history of the creation in the Bible—Berosheit. I want to analyze the word. Baith—in, by, through, and everything else. Rosh—the head. Sheit—grammatical termination. When the inspired man wrote it, he did not put the baith there. An old Jew without any authority added the word; he thought it too bad to begin to talk about the head! It read first, “The head one of the Gods brought forth the Gods.” That is the true meaning of the words. Baurau signifies to bring forth. If you do not believe it, you do not believe the learned man of God.13 Learned men can teach you no more than what I have told you. Thus the head God brought forth the Gods in the grand council.14

I will transpose and simplify it in the English language. Oh, ye lawyers, ye doctors, and ye priests,15 who have persecuted me, I want to let you know that the Holy Ghost knows something as well as you do.16 The head God called together the Gods and sat in grand council17 to bring forth the world. The grand councilors sat at the head in yonder heavens and con-
